staleness_policies.rationale: Trial registry records can change after initial ingestion, especially posted-results and status fields.
PubMed abstracts and metadata are comparatively stable but can receive corrections or indexing updates.
PubMed summary metadata is comparatively stable but can receive corrections or indexing updates.
Unknown source snapshot types use a conservative refresh interval until a source-specific policy exists.
Retained registry text should trail registry source-snapshot refresh checks but still age into a repair queue.
Normalized abstracts are stable enough for annual watch checks.
Retained full-text artifacts should be rechecked periodically for corrections, retractions, and rights changes.
Unknown text-snapshot scopes use a conservative refresh interval until a scope-specific policy exists.

coverage_gaps.description: DKD, IPF, and AD-risk human studies now have retained PubMed source snapshots supporting abstract-level sample, dosing, timepoint, effect, and safety details, and the legacy IPF generic feasibility/tolerability extraction has been reconciled to the N=12 randomized pilot. Full article/table extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements remains incomplete.
coverage_gaps.suggested_action: Retain or ingest full-text/table source snapshots for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements, then reconcile abstract-level extraction against article tables before formal synthesis.

coverage_gaps.description: Aggregate adverse-event counts, the D+Q bone headache preferred-term row, additional publication-table preferred-term rows, and registry/article discontinuation fields have been extracted for the D+Q bone RCT. IPF and AD-risk now have PubMed source-snapshot-backed abstract safety extraction, and DKD has a source-snapshot-backed record documenting that adverse-event counts were not reported in the retained abstract summary. Full article/table safety extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, D+Q bone supplement material, and harmonized cross-human adverse-event definitions remain incomplete.
coverage_gaps.suggested_action: Run retained full-text or table-row extraction for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety details, reconcile any D+Q bone supplement safety tables, and create a cross-human safety compatibility group only after event terms, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, and seriousness are harmonized.

coverage_gaps.description: Only two active no-results registry records are present. The prior coverage-repair session also deferred fisetin frailty registry candidates NCT03430037 and NCT03675724, but this batch could not fetch ClinicalTrials.gov source snapshots for those registries, so broader current fisetin and D+Q trial surveillance remains incomplete.
coverage_gaps.suggested_action: Run a source-snapshot-backed ClinicalTrials.gov surveillance pass for NCT03430037, NCT03675724, senolytics, D+Q, fisetin, and senescence-targeting terms; add no-results status records only when fetched registry snapshots support them.

coverage_gaps.description: No recent systematic-review or meta-analysis source has been screened into the knowledge base. This bounded batch did not close the gap because the job supplied no source row or context pack and PubMed/NLM retrieval was unavailable from the worker environment.
coverage_gaps.suggested_action: Run a bounded search/screening job for recent systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and trial-landscape reviews; create or refresh PubMed source snapshots before upgrading any review source beyond screened or triage-summary maturity.
